meta.source: Created from live source inspection in C:/Projects/qbo-escalations
meta.purpose: Human-readable system map plus machine-readable context for future LLM feature, bugfix, and review work.

dataContract.lanes: Ordered high-level architecture columns used by the visualization.
dataContract.nodes: System actors, client surfaces, routes, services, stores, and external dependencies.
dataContract.flows: User-visible or operational paths through the app. Each step references node ids and code evidence.

lanes.label: Actors
lanes.summary: People or trigger sources that start work.

lanes.label: Client Surfaces
lanes.summary: React views, panels, hooks, and API wrappers.

lanes.label: Orchestration & Agents
lanes.summary: Model routing, parsers, agents, workspace loops, and runtime guards.

lanes.label: Data Stores
lanes.summary: Mongo documents, local files, prompt/playbook content, and archives.

lanes.label: Connected Services
lanes.summary: Provider, Gmail, Calendar, and local/remote model dependencies.

lanes.label: Ops & Observability
lanes.summary: Tracing, usage, health, request visibility, and test harnesses.

nodes.title: /api/chat
nodes.subtitle: SSE send / retry / parallel turn routes

nodes.title: ImageParseResult + archive
nodes.subtitle: Parser result and source image history

flows.title: Screenshot to triage and chat answer
flows.intent: Turn an uploaded escalation screenshot into parsed fields, a triage card, INV context, a streamed answer, and a linked case record.

steps.label: send escalation screenshot
steps.detail: The phone agent gives the user a screenshot or template content that needs immediate triage.

steps.label: open parser surface
steps.detail: The client uses the chat image parser popup rather than posting images directly to main chat.

steps.label: POST /parse
steps.detail: The screenshot is sent to /api/image-parser/parse with provider, model, prompt, and timeout settings.

steps.label: parseImage()
steps.detail: The dedicated parser service invokes the configured image-capable provider and returns text, role, parse fields, and usage.

steps.label: provider call
steps.detail: Provider IDs are validated against llm-gateway, LM Studio, Anthropic, OpenAI, Kimi, and Gemini options.

steps.label: persist parse history
steps.detail: Successful and failed parses are saved as ImageParseResult records, with source images archived when available.

steps.label: send parsed text
steps.detail: Main chat receives text plus parsedEscalationText metadata. The current /api/chat route rejects raw images.

steps.label: augment image turn
steps.detail: buildChatImageAugmentation treats provided parser text as escalation context and prepares a triage-aware system prompt.

steps.label: search known INVs
steps.detail: Known Issue Search Agent can run before triage and passes retrieval evidence into the triage prompt.

steps.label: triage fields
steps.detail: The Triage Agent returns labeled fields for agent, client, category, severity, read, action, confidence, and category check.

steps.label: strong match fallback
steps.detail: If the known issue agent did not already produce matches, deterministic INV matching checks parsed fields and text.

steps.label: stream answer
steps.detail: The main chat orchestrator streams chunks, thinking, provider errors, fallback notices, status, triage_card, and inv_matches events.

steps.label: save turn
steps.detail: The user message and assistant response are persisted with provider, model, attempts, usage, thinking, and trace metadata.

steps.label: auto-link case
steps.detail: When valid parseFields exist, createLinkedEscalationFromConversation creates or reuses a linked Escalation.

steps.label: canonical record
steps.detail: The linked case stores COID, MID, case number, customer, category, status, source, parse metadata, and screenshots.

flows.title: Main chat text follow-up
flows.intent: Answer follow-up questions while preserving conversation context, provider attempts, traces, and quick actions.

steps.label: type follow-up
steps.detail: The user sends a text-only question or follow-up in ChatView.

steps.label: serialize + track
steps.detail: chatApi serializes JSON, uses apiFetch, reports local stages, and consumes SSE events.

steps.label: POST /api/chat
steps.detail: The chat route validates provider settings, conversation id, mode, model overrides, and input.

steps.label: prepare request
steps.detail: prepareChatRequest builds model context, applies provider strategy, estimates budget, and enforces guardrails.

steps.label: route provider
steps.detail: The orchestrator runs single, fallback, or parallel provider policies and streams output callbacks.

steps.label: execute model
steps.detail: Provider registry normalizes the selected model provider and exposes model ids for traces and usage.

steps.label: persist
steps.detail: The completed response is saved with usage, attempts, quick actions, and denormalized conversation metadata.

steps.label: trace + usage
steps.detail: Trace events, attempts, usage, fallback state, latency, and response stats are patched throughout the turn.

steps.label: SSE events
steps.detail: Client handlers update the thread, right dock, mini widget, triage card, INV banner, and errors.

flows.title: Parallel provider review
flows.intent: Run multiple providers for the same chat turn, keep candidate responses open, and let the user accept the canonical answer.

steps.label: mode=parallel
steps.detail: The client can request parallel mode with 2-4 unique providers.

steps.label: guard open turns
steps.detail: The server limits currently open parallel turns before accepting a new one.

steps.label: run candidates
steps.detail: Parallel provider results stream and are stored as candidate assistant messages.

steps.label: save candidates
steps.detail: Successful provider responses are normalized into candidate records for accept, unaccept, or discard.

steps.label: accept / discard
steps.detail: Client calls /api/chat/parallel/:turnId/accept, /discard, or /unaccept.

steps.label: canonical winner
steps.detail: Accepted content becomes the selected assistant message while candidate state remains auditable.

flows.title: Manual or text escalation to saved case
flows.intent: Create an Escalation record from pasted text, image, or an existing conversation using regex or provider-backed parsing.

steps.label: create or parse case
steps.detail: The dashboard and detail surfaces call the escalations API for create, update, parse, and link operations.

steps.label: POST /parse or /quick-parse
steps.detail: Quick mode is regex-only; other modes use provider-backed parse orchestration.

steps.label: parseWithPolicy()
steps.detail: Parser policy supports single, fallback, and parallel attempts, then validates normalized fields.

steps.label: provider attempt
steps.detail: AI parser providers are looked up through the common registry and catalog.

steps.label: dedupe + link
steps.detail: createLinkedEscalationFromConversation and duplicate safety logic prevent accidental duplicate records.

steps.label: persist case
steps.detail: The case stores parsed fields, parseMeta attempts, validation confidence, source, screenshots, and status.

steps.label: log parse trace
steps.detail: Parse attempts are recorded as AiTrace stages and UsageLog entries when an AI provider was used.

flows.title: Resolution to reviewed knowledge
flows.intent: Turn a finalized escalation into reusable reviewed knowledge and optionally publish it into the playbook.

steps.label: transition case
steps.detail: The client posts status transitions such as resolved or escalated-further.

steps.label: set status
steps.detail: Resolved cases get resolvedAt and optional resolution text.

steps.label: sync discipline
steps.detail: Resolution discipline and knowledge review attention items are synced around status and knowledge changes.

steps.label: generate knowledge
steps.detail: The client can generate a deterministic or enriched knowledge draft from an eligible case.

steps.label: save draft
steps.detail: KnowledgeCandidate captures title, symptom, root cause, exact fix, target, confidence, and source snapshot.

steps.label: approve + publish
steps.detail: Approved drafts publish into category or edge-case playbook markdown through knowledge-promotion helpers.

steps.label: write markdown
steps.detail: Published learning lands in repo playbook files and becomes future context for support answers.

flows.title: Attention center review queue
flows.intent: Surface workflow items that need the user's attention: duplicates, missing resolutions, stale cases, parse review, missing links, and knowledge review.

steps.label: open attention tab
steps.detail: The attention route renders EscalationDashboard with initialTab='attention'.

steps.label: GET /attention-items
steps.detail: The client can request refresh=1 to run all sync detectors before listing items.

steps.label: run detectors
steps.detail: The server syncs stale, parser triage, missing link, knowledge review, and resolution discipline items.

steps.label: upsert items
steps.detail: Attention items include kind, severity, fingerprint, source links, candidates, signals, and resolution state.

steps.label: review list
steps.detail: The dashboard receives populated source escalation, source conversation, candidate, count, and severity data.

steps.label: resolve / dismiss / split
steps.detail: Single or bulk PATCH routes update attention item status and optional resolution notes.

flows.title: INV import and escalation matching
flows.intent: Store known Intuit investigations and surface strong matches during screenshot triage and chat.

steps.label: add/search INV
steps.detail: The investigations UI supports search, stats, single records, and bulk import routes.

steps.label: POST /bulk or /
steps.detail: Imported investigation records include INV number, subject, agent, team, status, symptoms, workaround, and details.

steps.label: persist INV
steps.detail: The Investigation model indexes INV number, category, reported date, status, symptoms, and full-text fields.

steps.label: known issue retrieval
steps.detail: During screenshot triage, the Known Issue Search Agent may retrieve relevant investigations before the Triage Agent responds.

steps.label: deterministic fallback
steps.detail: Strong matches are filtered by exact confidence or score >= 40 and emitted as inv_matches SSE payloads.

steps.label: increment match
steps.detail: Matched investigations can update match count and last matched state without blocking chat.

steps.label: event: inv_matches
steps.detail: The client shows matched investigations so the user can add customers to affected-users lists.

flows.title: Workspace assistant action loop
flows.intent: Let the workspace assistant use context, connected-service state, memory, and tools while streaming progress back to the panel.

steps.label: ask workspace agent
steps.detail: WorkspaceShell and WorkspaceAgentPanel gather prompt, context, subview, and runtime selection.

steps.label: POST /ai
steps.detail: The workspace route rejects concurrent agent requests and streams start/status/chunk/action events.

steps.label: session + action flow
steps.detail: The server creates a workspace session, builds context, and runs action-capable workspace prompts.

steps.label: AI pass
steps.detail: Workspace provider policy supports single or fallback mode and uses the same common provider machinery.

steps.label: optional tools
steps.detail: Workspace tools can use Gmail and Calendar context when connected, with disconnected-service guardrails in the prompt.

steps.label: memory + activity
steps.detail: Workspace memory, entities, activity, alerts, patterns, and auto-actions are persisted under workspace models.

steps.label: SSE updates
steps.detail: The panel receives session id, phases, elapsed status ticks, actions, chunks, and completion data.

flows.title: Multi-agent room conversation
flows.intent: Coordinate multiple named agents in a shared room with mentions, memory capture, actions, realtime events, and interrupt handling.

steps.label: send room message
steps.detail: The room client sends message, parsed image context, system triggers, and agent runtime selections.

steps.label: POST /:id/send
steps.detail: The route validates room id, content, parsed image context, message length, and room existence.

steps.label: save user message
steps.detail: Messages include role, content, mentions, parsed image context, timestamp, and later agent response metadata.

steps.label: start orchestration
steps.detail: The room orchestrator parses mentions, chooses active agents, runs rounds, and emits per-agent callbacks.

steps.label: identity + memory
steps.detail: Agent profiles, social context, relationships, activity, and tool usage are attached to room behavior.

steps.label: room tools
steps.detail: Workspace-capable room agents can emit action groups and tool results for the shared room.

steps.label: SSE + realtime
steps.detail: The route streams room_start, agent_start, chunk, thinking, actions, status, agent_done, room_done, and interrupt events.

steps.label: persist agent turns
steps.detail: Agent responses, actions, iterations, usage, and shared room memory are saved back to ChatRoom.

flows.title: Gmail and calendar connected services
flows.intent: Connect Google accounts, read operational context, and expose email/calendar actions to the UI and assistant surfaces.

steps.label: open connected surface
steps.detail: GmailInbox and CalendarView provide inbox, labels, drafts, subscriptions, and event UI.

steps.label: auth + data routes
steps.detail: Routes cover auth status/url/callback/disconnect, accounts, messages, labels, drafts, send, and calendar events/freebusy.

steps.label: token state
steps.detail: GmailAuth stores connected accounts and selected account state used by Gmail service calls.

steps.label: Google API calls
steps.detail: The services layer performs Gmail and Calendar operations behind the route contract.

steps.label: workspace context
steps.detail: Workspace prompts include connected service status and warn agents not to fabricate email or calendar facts when disconnected.

flows.title: Provider settings and health checks
flows.intent: Configure model providers, validate image-parser keys, and surface runtime/provider health.

steps.label: edit provider strategy
steps.detail: Settings receives AI settings props and provider strategy controls from app shell hooks.

steps.label: status + keys
steps.detail: The image parser route exposes /status, /keys, PUT /keys, and POST /keys/test.

steps.label: availability cache
steps.detail: checkProviderAvailability and validateRemoteProvider test remote key and endpoint health.

steps.label: /v1/provider-status
steps.detail: llm-gateway is treated as an OpenAI-compatible provider for validation and parse/chat requests.

steps.label: runtime health
steps.detail: /api/runtime/health and /api/health/providers report request runtime, AI runtime, provider health, uptime, and node version.

steps.label: visible health
steps.detail: HealthBanner, HealthToast, and waterfall components surface request and provider problems without browser DevTools.

flows.title: Tracing, usage, and debug loop
flows.intent: Track every significant AI/API request from client waterfall through server traces, usage dashboards, and test harness coverage.

steps.label: watch client requests
steps.detail: apiFetch emits request lifecycle events used by the waterfall, health banner, and circuit indicators.

steps.label: request events
steps.detail: The client wrapper handles GET dedupe, timeout, retry, budget counters, circuit state, and stream-aware request phases.

steps.label: active operation
steps.detail: Chat, parse, and workspace routes register active AI operations and update runtime phase/chunks/events.

steps.label: trace record
steps.detail: AiTrace stores request, resolved policy, parse stages, triage stages, events, attempts, usage, and outcome.

steps.label: dashboards
steps.detail: Trace and usage routes expose summaries, recent runs, model trends, provider/service breakdowns, and conversation-level records.

steps.label: contract slices
steps.detail: Stress-testing slices and baselines cover main chat, image intake, escalation domain, connected services, room orchestration, workspace assistant, client surfaces, and runtime observability.

steps.label: error visibility
steps.detail: Express middleware normalizes API errors and reports server exceptions to the error pipeline for visibility.
